message:
fix: prevent graph analysis hang with HITS/cycle timeouts

The HITS algorithm and cycle detection could hang indefinitely on certain
graph structures (sparse, disconnected, or adversarial topologies). This
caused the app to get stuck on "Initializing..." at startup.

Changes:
- Wrap HITS algorithm call in a goroutine with 500ms timeout
- Wrap cycle detection (topo.DirectedCyclesIn) with 500ms timeout
- Relax HITS convergence tolerance from 1e-4 to 1e-3 for faster convergence
- Add comprehensive tests for timeout behavior:
  - TestAnalyzeCompletesWithinTimeout: 100-node sparse graph
  - TestAnalyzeNoEdgesGraph: nodes with no edges (HITS edge case)
  - TestAnalyzeSparseDisconnectedGraph: multiple disconnected components

If either algorithm times out, we gracefully skip those metrics rather
than blocking the UI. PageRank, Betweenness, and Eigenvector centrality
will still be computed (they have bounded iteration counts).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
